title: Rich Smoky Korean Pork Muchim
description: A smoky and savory Korean dish featuring pork, unsweetened coconut milk, and a variety of spices, perfect for a low-carb brunch
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit
In a large bowl, whisk together gochujang, soy sauce, brown sugar, smoked paprika, garlic, salt, and black pepper
Add the sliced pork to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade
Let the pork marinate for at least 30 minutes
Remove the pork from the marinade and cook in a skillet over medium-high heat until browned
Transfer the pork to a baking dish and bake in the oven for 20 minutes
While the pork is cooking, heat the sesame oil in a small saucepan over medium heat
Add the chopped green onions to the saucepan and cook until wilted
Stir in the unsweetened coconut milk and bring to a simmer
To assemble the muchim, place a portion of the cooked pork onto a plate, then spoon some of the coconut milk sauce over the top
Use your hands to shape the pork and sauce into small nigiri-shaped balls
